description: When configuring the same BGP neighbor under both IPv4 and IPv6 address families on a router, if a maximum prefix limit is set on one address family (like IPv4), exceeding that limit can potentially impact the BGP session for the other address family (IPv6) as well.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, what caveat is documented for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - When configuring the same BGP neighbor under both IPv4 and IPv6 address families on a router, if a maximum prefix limit is set on one address family (like IPv4), exceeding that limit can potentially impact the BGP session for the other address family (IPv6) as well.

description: If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, what caveat is documented for Activate?

Feature Caveat: Activate - If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms.

description: When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. This cycle will continue until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, it is best practice to disable HPE Aruba Networking Central until the switch is registered or a license is obtained.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, what caveat is documented for Central?

Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. This cycle will continue until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, it is best practice to disable HPE Aruba Networking Central until the switch is registered or a license is obtained.

description: Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, what caveat is documented for Classifiers?
Feature Caveat: Classifiers - Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

description: For secure classifier policy modifications, HPE Aruba Networking strongly encourages a three-step process: bring down the port, make the modifications, and then bring the port back up.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, what limitation is mentioned for Classifiers?

Feature Caveat: Classifiers - For secure classifier policy modifications, HPE Aruba Networking strongly encourages a three-step process: bring down the port, make the modifications, and then bring the port back up.

description: No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, what caveat is documented for CMF?
Feature Caveat: CMF - No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

description: When downloading a hot-patch file using the switch WebUI, log messages may incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, what caveat is documented for Hot Patch?

Feature Caveat: Hot Patch - When downloading a hot-patch file using the switch WebUI, log messages may incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

feature: IGMP/PIM on 6-in-6, Loopback and GRE interfaces
description: IGMP cannot be enabled on either Loopback or GRE interfaces. IGMP and PIM is not supported on a 6-in-6 Tunnel.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, what caveat is documented for IGMP/PIM on 6-in-6, Loopback and GRE interfaces?
Feature Caveat: IGMP/PIM on 6-in-6, Loopback and GRE interfaces - IGMP cannot be enabled on either Loopback or GRE interfaces. IGMP and PIM is not supported on a 6-in-6 Tunnel.

description: Do not use reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services for different services. When two services use the same port, unexpected behaviors may occur. Best practice is to assign a unique port for each service across the system.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, what caveat is documented for IP-SLA?

Feature Caveat: IP-SLA - Do not use reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services for different services. When two services use the same port, unexpected behaviors may occur. Best practice is to assign a unique port for each service across the system.

description: Multicast traffic cannot be run under GRE, 6in4, or 6in6 tunnels..

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, what caveat is documented for Multicast?
Feature Caveat: Multicast - Multicast traffic cannot be run under GRE, 6in4, or 6in6 tunnels.

description: REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, what caveat is documented for REST?
Feature Caveat: REST - REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

description: When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, what caveat is documented for SNMP?

Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

description: When configuring tunnels (VXLAN/IP tunnels) with the underlay as a static route, the next-hop IP should be an SVI or ROP IP and not configured as the Active-Gateway.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, what caveat is documented for Tunnels?
Feature Caveat: Tunnels - When configuring tunnels (VXLAN/IP tunnels) with the underlay as a static route, the next-hop IP should be an SVI or ROP IP and not configured as the Active-Gateway.

description: A GRE tunnel in an access-VLAN underlay is not supported in a non-default VRF.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, what caveat is documented for GRE?
Feature Caveat: GRE - A GRE tunnel in an access-VLAN underlay is not supported in a non-default VRF.

description: Overlay/system partition information is included in HOST-RESOURCES-MIB polling. The overlay partition is a read-only partition and this is reflected in SNMP queries. hrFSAccess.3 = readOnly(2) is expected behavior for overlay partitions. Monitoring tools should not treat this as an error.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, what limitation is mentioned for SNMP?

Feature Caveat: SNMP - Overlay/system partition information is included in HOST-RESOURCES-MIB polling. The overlay partition is a read-only partition and this is reflected in SNMP queries. hrFSAccess.3 = readOnly(2) is expected behavior for overlay partitions. Monitoring tools should not treat this as an error.

description: Symptom: ACL logging displays incorrect ACE sequence number and action while entries are being updated in hardware. Scenario: This issue occurs when an ACL entry is configured with the logging action the logged output may report an incorrect sequence number and action. Workaround: Wait for ACL programming to finish before utilizing logs for debug/analysis of traffic.

description: Symptom: MACs are only showing on one side of a VSX pair. For instance, a MAC is learned on a physical port on one VSX device and is missing from the peer VSX device. Scenario: This issue can occur after resolving a network loop which causes the MACs to quickly move from one VSX device to the other, particularly when MACs are moving from EVPN tunnels to physical ports. Workaround: Using the CLI to clear the problematic MAC address.

description: Symptom: hpe-restd crashes when attempting to obtain Central location from Activate. Scenario: This issue may occur if, the TLS handshake when connecting to Activate fails and causes a crash on the hpe-restd daemon (where Central connectivity resides). Workaround: DIsable the feature where Central is not being used and the crash is occuring.

description: Symptom: IP SLA track objects showing Down status even when tracked IP addresses are reachable and IP SLA sessions show UP status. Scenario: This issue may occur when there are significant timing differences between IPSLA probe intervals and track object delay timer values (for example a 5-second probe interval with 30-second delay timers).
symptom: IP SLA track objects showing Down status even when tracked IP addresses are reachable and IP SLA sessions show UP status.
scenario: This issue may occur when there are significant timing differences between IPSLA probe intervals and track object delay timer values (for example a 5-second probe interval with 30-second delay timers).

description: Symptom: Classifier Policy is not enforced for traffic coming from an ISL link. Scenario: For a topology with VSX+ VRRP, if the policy is applied on SVI, all packets which reach the VRRP secondary device go to the primary device via the ISL. All of those packets are not getting classifier policy enforced. Even though the policy action is PBR redirect, those packets are not getting redirected. Workaround: Two workarounds are possible to prevent packets from being forwarded to VSX primary via ISL. 1. Use active gateway to give better active active redundancy. Packets reaching either VSX device process the packet destined to active gateway. 2. Turn off the interface connection from VRRP secondary device to down switch. This method may cause redundancy and is not preferred.
symptom: Classifier Policy is not enforced for traffic coming from an ISL link.
scenario: For a topology with VSX+ VRRP, if the policy is applied on SVI, all packets which reach the VRRP secondary device go to the primary device via the ISL. All of those packets are not getting classifier policy enforced. Even though the policy action is PBR redirect, those packets are not getting redirected.
workaround: s are possible to prevent packets from being forwarded to VSX primary via ISL. 1. Use active gateway to give better active active redundancy. Packets reaching either VSX device process the packet destined to active gateway. 2. Turn off the interface connection from VRRP secondary device to down switch. This method may cause redundancy and is not preferred.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, what known issue is documented for PBR Bug 407418?

PBR (Bug ID 407418): Symptom: Classifier Policy is not enforced for traffic coming from an ISL link. Scenario: For a topology with VSX+ VRRP, if the policy is applied on SVI, all packets which reach the VRRP secondary device go to the primary device via the ISL. All of those packets are not getting classifier policy enforced. Even though the policy action is PBR redirect, those packets are not getting redirected. Workaround: Two workarounds are possible to prevent packets from being forwarded to VSX primary via ISL. 1. Use active gateway to give better active active redundancy. Packets reaching either VSX device process the packet destined to active gateway. 2. Turn off the interface connection from VRRP secondary device to down switch. This method may cause redundancy and is not preferred.

description: Symptom: VRRP is not supported on sub-interfaces and traffic loss is seen when VRRP is configured on sub-interface. Scenario: This issue may occur when VRRP is configured on sub-interfaces, traffic flow through the VRRP interfaces could get impacted. Workaround: It is recommended not to have VRRP configured on sub-interfaces.

description: Symptom: Traffic disruption occurs after globally removing a VLAN associated with both a VLAN Translation rule and VXLAN interface. Scenario: This issue occurs when following these steps: 1. Create a VLAN and associating to VXLAN and a host connected port. 2. On a host connected port, configure VLAN translate 210 to 410. 3. Then delete VLAN 410 globally. Workaround: Reconfigure vlan trunk allow 210 on a host connected port.

description: Symptom: Interface VXLAN configuration is disabled on 9300 and 9300S platforms for spine & DC-Optimized-Spine profiles from 10.15 onwards. Scenario: This issue may occur if using Spine profile with VXLAN configurations before 10.15 release. Workaround: Change to Leaf profile and perform switch reboot before upgrading to 10.15 or higher AOS-CX versions.
